item.title: 假期后拉取请求 | Post-Vacation Pull Request
item.description:
多米尼克·R。

发表于 6 月 29 日

所以我刚刚放假回家。

还有什么比为开源项目做出贡献更好的方式让我轻松回到正常生活呢？

问题是，通常像我这样有能力的人能解决的一切都已经被抢购一空了。

你猜怎么了？ 我发现了一个未受影响的存储库，所需的代码贡献绝对为零。 不客气。

我提出过要求，但我从不提出要求。 就是这么简单。

请在此处查看有关对开源产生影响的原始帖子。

快乐贡献！ 阿卜杜拉·穆宾 - 6 月 11 日

一旦被暂停，jd2r 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到其暂停被解除。

一旦解封，jd2r 将可以再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，jd2r 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有自己可以访问。

如果 jd2r 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从仪表板重新发布帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保证 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 jd2r：

使 jd2r 的所有帖子不那么明显

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 jd2r 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Dominic R.

Posted on Jun 29

 

So I just got home from vacation.

What better way to ease back into my normal life than to contribute to an open source project?

The issue with that is that usually everything that someone of my abilities could tackle has already been snapped up.

Guess what? I found an untouched repository with absolutely zero code contributions required. You're welcome.

I made a request, and I never make requests. It's that easy.

Go check out the original post about making an impact in open source here.

Happy contributing! ✌

Abdullah al Mubin - Jun 11

Once suspended, jd2r will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, jd2r will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by jd2r will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If jd2r is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Report other inappropriate conduct

item.author: jd2r

item.title: 通过 QUIC 上的 MQTT 克服地址更改 | Overcoming Address Change with MQTT over QUIC
item.description:
发表于 6 月 29 日

介绍

在这篇文章中，我们将概述QUIC 上的 MQTT ，并解释当 TCP 客户端遇到地址迁移时会发生什么。 然后我们将其与QUIC 协议所表现出的行为进行比较。

使用 MQTT over TCP 时，网络地址更改是指客户端 IP 地址在活动连接期间发生更改的情况。 发生这种情况的原因有多种，例如设备在网络之间移动、从 Wi-Fi 切换到蜂窝数据或遇到网络中断。

基于 TCP 的 MQTT 连接中的网络地址更改带来了挑战，并可能导致连接断开和重新建立。 客户端源地址的地址更改在现代移动网络中尤其常见。 当移动设备移动或遇到不同的网络条件时，移动设备会频繁地在不同的网络类型（例如 Wi-Fi、4G 或 5G）之间切换。

这种动态行为为基于 TCP 连接的 MQTT 添加了额外的复杂性。 此外，地址迁移事件会给MQTT 代理带来额外的负载，从而难以有效地规划硬件资源。 由于地址更改而导致的突然重新连接可能会导致 MQTT 代理的容量紧张并影响其性能。 确保 MQTT 代理能够处理大量重新连接请求成为此类场景中的关键考虑因素。 此外，从开发的角度来看，在测试环境中模拟客户端源地址的变化并不是一项简单的任务。 它通常需要专门的工具和配置来准确模拟移动网络的动态特性。 这种复杂性使得开发人员在面对网络地址更改时彻底测试 MQTT 应用程序的行为和稳健性具有挑战性。

与 MQTT over TCP 相比，MQTT over QUIC 提供了更有效的解决方案来解决地址更改的障碍。 通过利用 QUIC 传输，可以观察到以下好处：

  • 地址迁移弹性：QUIC 使客户端能够无缝迁移其 IP 地址，从而最大限度地减少 MQTT 连接的中断。 由于地址更改而导致的重新连接尝试和会话重新建立的负担显着减少。
  • 减少 MQTT 代理的负载：MQTT 通过 QUIC 更有效地处理地址更改的能力减轻了 MQTT 代理的负载。 开发简单性：基于 QUIC 的 MQTT 通过提供对地址迁移的内置支持，简化了开发和测试过程。 开发人员可以更多地关注应用程序逻辑和功能，而不必过多担心测试期间模拟地址更改。

通过在 QUIC 上采用 MQTT，物联网应用程序可以受益于改进的可扩展性、减少 MQTT 代理的负载以及在客户端源地址发生更改时简化的开发和测试流程。

演示：通过 QUIC 进行 MQTT 中的地址/连接迁移

在本章中，我们将引导您逐步演示 MQTT over QUIC 中的客户端地址迁移功能。 我们假设您对MQTT有基本的了解，并对 QUIC 的概念有一定的了解。

让我们深入了解演示。

  1. 设置 MQTT over QUIC 环境。
  • 首先使用GitHub - emqx/cdk-emqx-cluster上提供的 AWS CDK 脚本配置测试集群。
  • 按照存储库中的说明在您的 AWS 环境中部署集群。
  • 集群设置包括一个 EMQX MQTT 代理、两个负载生成器和监控服务。
  • 本演示中使用的 EMQX MQTT 服务器版本是开源的 5.0.26。
  • 使用 emqtt-bench 工具将负载生成器配置为支持 QUIC 协议。

​

 CDK_EMQX_CLUSTERNAME=william cdk deploy CdkEmqxClusterStack -c retain_efs=FALSE -c emqx_n=1 -c lg_n=2 -c emqx_ins_type="m5.xlarge" -c loadgen_ins_type="m5.xlarge" --parameters sshkey=qzhuyan -c emqx_src="wget https://github.com/emqx/emqx/releases/download/v5.0.26/emqx-5.0.26-ubuntu20.04-amd64.deb" Outputs: CdkEmqxClusterStack.BastionBastionHostId8F8CEB82 = i-013c898310b5e2f41 CdkEmqxClusterStack.ClusterName = william CdkEmqxClusterStack.EFSID = fs-006b96ed41efd7efd CdkEmqxClusterStack.ExportsOutputRefVPCEMQXwilliamA0A7F0C5ED461C91 = vpc-0bc7e7f3261e0ac59 CdkEmqxClusterStack.ExportsOutputRefVPCEMQXwilliamPrivateSubnet1SubnetDAC171FE82109DBA = subnet-00967d6e2a803ca98 CdkEmqxClusterStack.Hostsare = emqx-0.int.william etcd0.int.william etcd1.int.william etcd2.int.william loadgen-0.int.william loadgen-1.int.william CdkEmqxClusterStack.Loadbalancer = emqx-nlb-william-dd5d78ce97949e12.elb.eu-north-1.amazonaws.com CdkEmqxClusterStack.MonitoringGrafana = lb.int.william:3000 CdkEmqxClusterStack.MonitoringPostgresPassword = william CdkEmqxClusterStack.MonitoringPrometheus = lb.int.william:9090 CdkEmqxClusterStack.SSHCommandsforAccess = ssh -A -l ec2-user 16.16.202.211 -L 8888:lb.int.william:80 -L 13000:lb.int.william:3000 -L 19090:lb.int.william:9090 -L 15432:lb.int.william:5432 -L 28083:lb.int.william:8083 2>/dev/null CdkEmqxClusterStack.SSHEntrypoint = 16.16.202.211
  • 在分配给发布者的主机上，打开两个单独的终端或命令提示符窗口。
  • 在一个终端中，通过运行适当的命令来启动使用 TCP 传输的发布程序。 该发布者将向主题“/over/tcp”发布消息，绑定到本地地址192.168.0.200

​

 ./emqtt_bench pub -h emqx-0.int.william -t '/over/tcp' -c 1 --ifaddr 192.168.0.200 -q 1 --prefix PubTCP
  • 在另一个终端中，使用以下命令启动使用 QUIC 传输的发布程序。 该发布者将向主题“/over/quic”发布消息，绑定到本地地址192.168.0.200

​

 ./emqtt_bench pub -h emqx-0.int.william -t '/over/quic' --quic -p 14567 -c 1 --ifadd r 192.168.0.200 -q 1 --prefix PubQUIC
  • 在分配给订阅者的主机上，打开两个单独的终端或命令提示符窗口。
  • 在一个终端中，通过运行必要的脚本或命令来启动 TCP 订阅者。 该订阅者将订阅主题“/over/tcp”，QoS 1

​

 ./emqtt_bench sub -h emqx-0.int.william -t '/over/tcp' -c 1 -q 1

  • 在另一个终端中，使用适当的脚本或命令启动 QUIC 订阅者。

    • 该订阅者将订阅主题“/over/quic”，QoS 1

​

 ./emqtt_bench sub -h emqx-0.int.william -t '/over/quic -c 1 -q 1
  • 观察订阅者的终端并确保他们开始接收来自各自主题的消息。
  • 监视控制台输出以查看“/over/tcp”和“/over/quic”的接收消息速率。

  • 监控 EMQX 控制台，对端客户端源 IP 地址为 192.168.0.200。 触发 NAT 重新绑定。
    • 通过触发 NAT 重新绑定事件来模拟客户端源地址更改，该事件将 TCP 和 UDP (QUIC) 协议的客户端源地址从 192.168.0.200 更改为 192.168.0.203。 当地址迁移事件发生时，观察 QUIC 连接上的 MQTT 的行为。
    1. 验证地址迁移。
    • 监控订阅者的终端，检查地址迁移后是否继续接收消息。

    • 验证“/over/quic”主题的接收消息速率是否保持不间断。

    • 监控 EMQX 控制台，对端客户端源 IP 地址为 192.168.0.203。

    1. 恢复到旧的 src 地址。 这模拟客户端切换回旧网络并取回旧源地址。

      拆除测试环境。

      在终端中执行以下命令来拆除测试集群：

按照演示中的详细步骤，您可以全面演示 MQTT over QUIC 中的客户端地址迁移功能。 测试集群的配置确保了适合测试地址更改期间 MQTT 连接行为的环境。 启动发布者和订阅者可以让您观察消息流和接收消息速率。 最后，触发 NAT 重新绑定事件模拟客户端源地址更改，使您能够验证 MQTT over QUIC 的地址/连接迁移能力。

结论

在这篇博文中，我们探讨了 MQTT 中基于 QUIC 的客户端地址迁移功能及其在处理活动连接期间地址更改方面的优势。 我们讨论了 MQTT over TCP 在遇到地址迁移时面临的挑战，包括连接丢失和重新建立，以及 MQTT 代理的额外负载。 我们还承认在测试环境中模拟地址更改的复杂性。

基于 QUIC 的 MQTT 成为应对这些挑战的强大解决方案。 通过利用 QUIC 协议的功能，基于 QUIC 的 MQTT 提供无缝地址迁移弹性、减少 MQTT 代理的负载并简化开发和测试流程。 事实证明，该协议在客户端源地址更改普遍存在的现代移动网络中特别有价值。

通过分步演示，我们展示了 MQTT 相对于 QUIC 在处理客户端地址迁移方面的有效性。 通过在 QUIC 上采用 MQTT，开发人员和组织可以增强基于 MQTT 的系统的可靠性、可扩展性和整体性能。

随着物联网应用在动态网络环境中不断发展，基于 QUIC 的 MQTT 因其无缝处理地址迁移的能力而被证明是一种有价值的协议选择。 在EMQX作为第一个通过 QUIC 支持 MQTT 的 MQTT 代理的努力下，我们可以期待在解决网络相关挑战以及在不断扩展的物联网世界中实现无缝通信方面取得进一步进展。

最初发布于www.emqx.com

一旦暂停，emqx 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到其暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，emqx 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，emqx 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 emqx 没有暂停，他们仍然可以从仪表板重新发布帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保证 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 emqx：

使 emqx 的所有帖子不那么明显

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 emqx 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

EMQ Technologies for EMQ Technologies

Posted on Jun 29

Introduction

In this post, we will provide an overview of MQTT over QUIC and explain what happens when a TCP client encounters address migration. We will then compare this with the behavior exhibited by the QUIC protocol.

MQTT over TCP: Challenges of Network Address Change

When using MQTT over TCP, network address change refers to a situation where the client's IP address changes during an active connection. This can occur due to various reasons, such as a device moving between networks, switching from Wi-Fi to cellular data, or experiencing network disruptions.

Network address change in TCP-based MQTT connections presents challenges and may result in connection drops and re-establishment. Address changes in client source addresses are particularly common in modern mobile networks. Mobile devices frequently switch between different network types, such as Wi-Fi, 4G, or 5G, as they move or encounter varying network conditions.

This dynamic behavior adds an extra layer of complexity to MQTT over TCP connections. Additionally, address migration events can put an extra load on the MQTT broker, making it difficult to plan hardware resources efficiently. Sudden bursts of reconnections due to address changes can strain the MQTT broker's capacity and affect its performance. Ensuring that the MQTT broker can handle a high volume of reconnection requests becomes a crucial consideration in such scenarios. Furthermore, from a development perspective, simulating client source address changes in a test environment is not a trivial task. It often requires specialized tools and configurations to mimic the dynamic nature of mobile networks accurately. This complexity makes it challenging for developers to thoroughly test the behavior and robustness of their MQTT applications in the face of the network address change.

MQTT over QUIC: A Better Solution to Address Change Scenarios

MQTT over QUIC provides a more efficient solution for tackling the obstacles of address change compared to MQTT over TCP. The QUIC protocol is designed to handle network changes seamlessly, providing enhanced resilience.

By leveraging QUIC transport, the following benefits can be observed:

  • Address Migration Resilience: QUIC enables clients to migrate their IP addresses seamlessly, minimizing disruptions in the MQTT connection. The burden of reconnection attempts and session re-establishment due to address changes is significantly reduced.
  • Reduced Load on MQTT Broker: The ability of MQTT over QUIC to handle address change more efficiently alleviates the load on the MQTT broker. The MQTT broker can better manage resource allocation and scaling plans, taking into account the reduced impact of sudden bursts of reconnections.
  • Development Simplicity: MQTT over QUIC simplifies the development and testing process by providing built-in support for address migration. Developers can focus more on the application logic and functionality without worrying extensively about simulating address changes during testing.

By adopting MQTT over QUIC, IoT applications can benefit from improved scalability, reduced load on the MQTT broker, and simplified development and testing processes in the presence of client source address changes.

Demo: Address/Connection Migration in MQTT over QUIC

In this chapter, we will walk you through a step-by-step demonstration of the client address migration feature in MQTT over QUIC. We will assume that you have a basic understanding of MQTT and some familiarity with the concept of QUIC.

Let's dive into the demo.

  1. Set up the MQTT over QUIC environment.
  • Begin by provisioning the test cluster using the provided AWS CDK script available at GitHub - emqx/cdk-emqx-cluster.
  • Follow the instructions in the repository to deploy the cluster in your AWS environment.
  • The cluster setup includes an EMQX MQTT broker, two load generators, and monitoring services.
  • The EMQX MQTT broker version used in this demo is open-source 5.0.26.
  • The load generators are configured to support the QUIC protocol using the emqtt-bench tool.

​

CDK_EMQX_CLUSTERNAME=william cdk deploy CdkEmqxClusterStack  -c retain_efs=FALSE  -c emqx_n=1 -c lg_n=2 -c emqx_ins_type="m5.xlarge" -c loadgen_ins_type="m5.xlarge" --parameters sshkey=qzhuyan -c emqx_src="wget https://github.com/emqx/emqx/releases/download/v5.0.26/emqx-5.0.26-ubuntu20.04-amd64.deb"

Outputs:
CdkEmqxClusterStack.BastionBastionHostId8F8CEB82 = i-013c898310b5e2f41
CdkEmqxClusterStack.ClusterName = william
CdkEmqxClusterStack.EFSID = fs-006b96ed41efd7efd
CdkEmqxClusterStack.ExportsOutputRefVPCEMQXwilliamA0A7F0C5ED461C91 = vpc-0bc7e7f3261e0ac59
CdkEmqxClusterStack.ExportsOutputRefVPCEMQXwilliamPrivateSubnet1SubnetDAC171FE82109DBA = subnet-00967d6e2a803ca98
CdkEmqxClusterStack.Hostsare = emqx-0.int.william
etcd0.int.william
etcd1.int.william
etcd2.int.william
loadgen-0.int.william
loadgen-1.int.william
CdkEmqxClusterStack.Loadbalancer = emqx-nlb-william-dd5d78ce97949e12.elb.eu-north-1.amazonaws.com
CdkEmqxClusterStack.MonitoringGrafana = lb.int.william:3000
CdkEmqxClusterStack.MonitoringPostgresPassword = william
CdkEmqxClusterStack.MonitoringPrometheus = lb.int.william:9090
CdkEmqxClusterStack.SSHCommandsforAccess = ssh -A -l ec2-user 16.16.202.211 -L 8888:lb.int.william:80 -L 13000:lb.int.william:3000 -L 19090:lb.int.william:9090 -L 15432:lb.int.william:5432 -L 28083:lb.int.william:8083 2>/dev/null
CdkEmqxClusterStack.SSHEntrypoint = 16.16.202.211
  • On the host assigned to the publishers, open two separate terminals or command prompt windows.
  • In one terminal, start the publisher using TCP transport by running the appropriate command. This publisher will publish messages to the topic '/over/tcp', binding to the local address 192.168.0.200

​

./emqtt_bench pub -h emqx-0.int.william  -t '/over/tcp'  -c 1 --ifaddr 192.168.0.200 -q 1 --prefix PubTCP
  • In the other terminal, start the publisher using QUIC transport using the command. This publisher will publish messages to the topic '/over/quic', binding to the local address 192.168.0.200

​

./emqtt_bench pub -h emqx-0.int.william  -t '/over/quic' --quic -p 14567 -c 1 --ifadd r 192.168.0.200 -q 1 --prefix PubQUIC
  • On the host assigned to the subscribers, open two separate terminals or command prompt windows.
  • In one terminal, start the subscriber for TCP by running the necessary script or command. This subscriber will subscribe to the topic '/over/tcp', QoS 1

​

./emqtt_bench sub -h emqx-0.int.william  -t '/over/tcp' -c 1  -q 1

  • In the other terminal, start the subscriber for QUIC using the appropriate script or command.

    • This subscriber will subscribe to the topic '/over/quic', QoS 1

​

./emqtt_bench sub -h emqx-0.int.william  -t '/over/quic  -c 1  -q 1
  • emqtt_bench prints the receiving message rate on the console.

  • Observe the terminals for the subscribers and ensure that they start receiving messages from the respective topics.
  • Monitor the console output to view the receiving message rates for '/over/tcp' and '/over/quic'.

  • Monitor the EMQX console that the peer client src IP address is from 192.168.0.200. sudo tcpdump -i ens5 -n net 192.168.0.0/24

  1. Trigger the NAT rebinding.
  • Simulate the client source address change by triggering a NAT rebinding event that changes client src address from 192.168.0.200 to 192.168.0.203 for both TCP and UDP (QUIC) protocol. sudo iptables -t nat -I POSTROUTING -s 192.168.0.200 -j SNAT --to-source 192.168.0.203 sudo conntrack -F
  • Observe the behavior of the MQTT over QUIC connections as the address migration event occurs.
  1. Validate the address migration.
  • Monitor the terminals for the subscribers and check that they continue to receive messages after the address migration.

  • Verify that the receiving message rates for '/over/quic' topics are maintained without interruption.

  • Monitor the EMQX console that the peer client src IP address is from 192.168.0.203.

  1. Revert back to the old src address. This simulates the client switch back to the old network and get back the old source address.

    sudo iptables -F -t nat

  1. Tear down the test environment.

    Execute the following command in your terminal to tear down the test cluster:

CDK_EMQX_CLUSTERNAME=william cdk destroy CdkEmqxClusterStack -c retain_efs=FALSE -cemqx_n=1 -c lg_n=2 -c emqx_ins_type="m5.xlarge" -c loadgen_ins_type="m5.xlarge" --parameters sshkey=qzhuyan -c emqx_src="wget github.com/emqx/emqx/releases/download/…"

By following these detailed steps in the demo, you can conduct a comprehensive demonstration of the client address migration feature in MQTT over QUIC. The test cluster provision ensures an environment suitable for testing the behavior of MQTT connections during address changes. Starting the publishers and subscribers allows you to observe the message flow and receiving message rates. Finally, triggering the NAT rebinding event simulates the client source address change, enabling you to validate the address/connection migration capabilities of MQTT over QUIC.

Conclusion

In this blog post, we explored the client address migration feature in MQTT over QUIC and its benefits in handling address changes during active connections. We discussed the challenges faced by MQTT over TCP when encountering address migration, including connection drops and reestablishment, as well as the additional load on MQTT brokers. We also acknowledged the complexity of simulating address changes in testing environments.

MQTT over QUIC emerged as a powerful solution to address these challenges. By leveraging the capabilities of the QUIC protocol, MQTT over QUIC offers seamless address migration resilience, reduced load on MQTT brokers, and simplified development and testing processes. This protocol proves particularly valuable in modern mobile networks, where client source address changes are prevalent.

Through the step-by-step demonstration, we showcased the effectiveness of MQTT over QUIC in handling client address migration. By adopting MQTT over QUIC, developers and organizations can enhance the reliability, scalability, and overall performance of their MQTT-based systems.

As IoT applications continue to evolve in dynamic network environments, MQTT over QUIC proves to be a valuable protocol choice with its ability to handle address migration seamlessly. With the effort of EMQX as the first MQTT broker that supports MQTT over QUIC, we can expect further advancements in addressing network related challenges and enabling seamless communication in the ever-expanding world of IoT.

Originally published at www.emqx.com

Once suspended, emqx will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, emqx will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by emqx will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If emqx is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Make all posts by emqx less visible

Report other inappropriate conduct
